Package org.eventb.internal.core.ast.datatype

Class TypeSubstitution

java.lang.Object
org.eventb.internal.core.ast.TypeRewriter
org.eventb.internal.core.ast.datatype.TypeSubstitution
All Implemented Interfaces:
ITypeInstantiation, ITypeVisitor
public class TypeSubstitution extends TypeRewriter implements ITypeInstantiation
Represents an instantiation of a datatype, where type parameters are associated with some arbitrary types. Instances are intended to be used for performing type substitution on datatype extensions.

  • Constructor Details

  • Method Details

    • makeSubstitution

      public static TypeSubstitution makeSubstitution(Datatype datatype, ITypeCheckMediator tcMediator)
      Returns a substitution appropriate for type-checking. All actual type parameters are type variables.
      Parameters:
      datatype - a datatype
      tcMediator - a type-checking mediator
      Returns:
      a type rewriter that can be applied to constructor arguments

    • makeSubstitution

      public static TypeSubstitution makeSubstitution(Datatype datatype, Type proposedInstance)
      Returns a substitution appropriate for verifying types. The actual type parameters are inferred from the proposed type, if possible. This method must not throw any exception as it is called during type-checking.
      Parameters:
      datatype - a datatype
      proposedInstance - a proposed instance of the datatype type
      Returns:
      a type rewriter that can be applied to constructor arguments, or null if the proposed type is invalid

    • getOrigin

      public IDatatype getOrigin()
      Description copied from interface: ITypeInstantiation
      Returns the datatype that built this type instantiation.
      Specified by:
      getOrigin in interface ITypeInstantiation
      Returns:
      the datatype of this type instantiation

    • getInstanceType

      public ParametricType getInstanceType()
      Description copied from interface: ITypeInstantiation
      Returns the type from which this instance was built, that is a type built with the type constructor of the datatype of this instance.
      Specified by:
      getInstanceType in interface ITypeInstantiation
      Returns:
      the type used to build this instance

      Visits the given "given type" node.
